channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Northern Ireland emergency departments face record pressures and critical staffing challenges
item.description: Emergency departments across Northern Ireland are experiencing unprecedented strain, with senior doctors warning that staff are increasingly forced to make difficult decisions about which critically i...

item.title: Cremation's Rise Reveals Complex Preferences in End-of-Life Decisions
item.description: Nearly two-thirds of Americans now opt for cremation, but a closer look reveals that many aren't choosing it out of preference. A 2024 survey on death care methods found that while cremation is popula...

item.title: CJP Raises Concerns Over Transfer of Judges Back to SHC, Citing Constitutional Issues
item.description: Chief Justice of Pakistan (CJP) Yahya Afridi has expressed concerns about the proposed transfer of Justice Khadim Hussain Soomro from the Islamabad High Court (IHC) to the Sindh High Court (SHC). In h...

item.title: Kenya and Ghana Partner to Align Digital Asset Policy and Build Cross-Regional Collaboration
item.description: Kenya and Ghana have taken a significant step towards strengthening Africa’s digital asset space by signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to form what they are calling an East–West African Digit...

item.title: Kailyn Lowry's Family and Her Four Baby Daddies
item.description: Kailyn Lowry, the American reality TV personality and podcast host, has had four baby daddies: Jo Rivera, Javi Marroquin, Chris Lopez, and Elijah Scott. She became a teenage mother in January 2010, gi...

item.title: Raghav Chadha's Exit from AAP: Reasons Behind His Departure with 6 Other MPs
item.description: In a significant move, Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha, along with six other MPs, left the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) and merged with the BJP, dealing a blow to Arvind Kejriwal's party. The seven MPs, mostly ...

item.title: Halifax Harbour's Marine Life Documented by Local Underwater Photographer
item.description: A dedicated underwater photographer in Halifax Harbour has been capturing vibrant images of the diverse marine life inhabiting the area. The photographer, who has extensive experience exploring local ...

item.title: Norway Set To Ban Social Media Access For Children Under 16
item.description: Norway is about to introduce a bill that will prohibit children under the age of 16 from using social media. This new legislation aims to safeguard children’s digital lives, shifting the responsibilit...

item.title: Owen Farrell highlights Noah Caluori’s all-round performance as Saracens secure record win
item.description: Owen Farrell has praised teenage winger Noah Caluori for his exceptional physicality, confidence, and all-round ability as he continues a breakout season with Saracens. The 19-year-old has scored five...

item.title: Rising Insecurity Forces Over 3.7 Million Nigerians Into Internal Displacement
item.description: Nigeria dey face serious displacement crisis as new data from UNHCR show say over 3.7 million people don become internally displaced as of March 2026. These people, wey dem call IDPs, na those wey run...

item.title: Ryan Thomas reflects on early Coronation Street behaviour after co-star's advice
item.description: Ryan Thomas, known for his role as Jason Grimshaw on Coronation Street from 2000 to 2016, opened up about receiving criticism from a former co-star for displaying 'cocky behaviour' on set. Speaking on...

item.title: Why Tech Layoffs at Meta and Microsoft Are Only Partly About AI
item.description: Meta and Microsoft have announced significant job cuts while simultaneously ramping up investment in artificial intelligence, prompting questions about whether AI is truly driving these layoffs. Meta ...

item.title: The Growing Crisis of Violence Against South African Learners
item.description: South African schoolchildren are increasingly at risk from violent crime, with recent incidents highlighting the dire situation in both Gauteng and the Western Cape. In Gauteng, learners such as 16-ye...

item.title: US seizes Iranian ship carrying missile-making chemicals from China
item.description: The United States Navy recently intercepted an Iranian-flagged vessel named TOUSKA in the Gulf of Oman. According to Pentagon reports, the ship was transporting chemicals from China that can be used t...

item.title: Army and Police Investigate Dawlah Islamiya's Link to Jogger Ambush in Lanao del Norte
item.description: The fatal ambush of two joggers in Kolambugan, Lanao del Norte on April 21, 2026, has led authorities to investigate whether remnants of the defunct Dawlah Islamiya group were behind the attack. The t...

item.title: Women-Owned Businesses Dominate Government Procurement But Face Barriers to Full Participation
item.description: A recent report shows that women-led businesses in Kenya secured 58.3% of government contracts under the Access to Government Procurement Opportunities (AGPO) program in the financial year ending June...

item.title: Mynt CEO highlights fintech and government support in helping consumers cope with rising fuel costs
item.description: In the Philippines, rising fuel prices, inflation, and economic volatility have created a triple challenge for consumers. At CNBC’s CONVERGE LIVE 2026 in Singapore, Martha Sazon, CEO of Mynt—the paren...

item.title: Mumbai woman faces complaint after requesting BJP protest to move to designated ground
item.description: In Mumbai, a complaint has been filed against a woman who intervened during a BJP-led protest that caused traffic congestion in the Worli area. The protest, organised by the ruling Mahayuti coalition,...

item.title: Father and toddler daughter share joyful mountain bike rides online
item.description: Gareth Williams, a 33-year-old father from Blaenau Gwent, has delighted millions with videos of himself and his toddler daughter, Indie, enjoying mountain bike rides together. Indie, who loves scootin...

item.title: World Cup 2026: Public Transport Costs and Offers for Fans Across Host Cities
item.description: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup approaches, football fans attending matches in the United States will face a wide range of public transport costs depending on the city. New Jersey, hosting the crucial Engl...
